description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages compile, package, test, staging, save-to-cubi and sonar. In the compile stage add linux-386-signed, linux-amd64-signed, linux-armel-signed, linux-armhf-signed, windows-386-signed and windows-amd64-signed jobs that use the golang:1.10 image, run only on tags or schedules, and execute the script: NIGHTLY=$(./_scripts/ci/test_if_nightly_build_required.sh); if [[ $NIGHTLY == "abort" ]]; then exit 0; fi; if [[ $GOOS == "windows" ]]; then apt-get update && apt install -y zip; fi; time go build -ldflags "-X gitlab.com/stp-team/systemtestportal-webapp/config.version=$(./_scripts/ci/get_version.sh)" -o $BIN_NAME ./cmd/stp; chmod u+x $BIN_NAME; pack_cmd=$(if [[ $GOOS == "windows" ]]; then echo "zip -r"; else echo "tar -cvzf"; fi); $pack_cmd $CI_PROJECT_DIR/$FILE_NAME templates static migrations data $BIN_NAME README.md; echo "$STP_GPG_PRIVATE" > stp-private.key; gpg --allow-secret-key-import --import stp-private.key; gpg --detach-sign $CI_PROJECT_DIR/$FILE_NAME; echo "$STP_GPG_PUBLIC" > stp-public.key; gpg --import stp-public.key; gpg --verify $CI_PROJECT_DIR/${FILE_NAME}.sig $CI_PROJECT_DIR/$FILE_NAME, producing artifacts named $FILE_NAME and ${FILE_NAME}.sig. Add a linux-amd64-unsigned compile job that uses the same image but runs the unsigned script: echo $BIN_NAME; echo $FILE_NAME; time go build -ldflags "-X gitlab.com/stp-team/systemtestportal-webapp/config.version=$(./_scripts/ci/get_version.sh)" -o stp ./cmd/stp; chmod u+x stp; pack_cmd=$(if [[ $GOOS == "windows" ]]; then echo "zip -r"; else echo "tar -cvzf"; fi); mkdir $BIN_NAME; mv templates $BIN_NAME/; mv static $BIN_NAME/; mv migrations $BIN_NAME/; mv data $BIN_NAME/; mv stp $BIN_NAME/$BIN_NAME; mv README.md $BIN_NAME/; mv LICENSE $BIN_NAME/; $pack_cmd $CI_PROJECT_DIR/$FILE_NAME $BIN_NAME; rm -R $BIN_NAME, also producing the same artifacts. In the package stage add deb-386, deb-amd64, deb-armel and deb-armhf jobs that use golang:1.10, run only on tags or schedules, depend on xgo, and execute: NIGHTLY=$(./_scripts/ci/test_if_nightly_build_required.sh); if [[ $NIGHTLY == "abort" ]]; then exit 0; fi; VERSION=$(./_scripts/ci/get_version.sh); SUITE=$(./_scripts/ci/get_suite.sh $CI_COMMIT_TAG); cp ./_scripts/ci/*.sh $CI_PROJECT_DIR; cd $CI_PROJECT_DIR; mkdir -p "$NAME/DEBIAN"; tar -xvzf "${NAME}-linux-${ARCH}_${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}.tar.gz"; ./service_tmpl.sh /usr/bin /usr/share/stp > stp.service; ./config_tmpl.sh /usr/share/stp /var/stp > config.ini; mkdir -p ${NAME}/var/stp; ./deb_control_tmpl.sh $NAME $VERSION ${ARCH} > "${NAME}/DEBIAN/control"; ./install_stp.sh . ${NAME}/usr/bin ${NAME}/usr/share/stp ${NAME}/etc/systemd/system ${NAME}/etc/stp; time dpkg-deb --build $NAME; echo $SUITE > suite; echo "$STP_GPG_PRIVATE" > stp-private.key; gpg --allow-secret-key-import --import stp-private.key; gpg --detach-sign $NAME.deb, producing artifacts named ${NAME}_${ARCH}-deb, ${NAME}.deb, ${NAME}.deb.sig and suite. Add docker-signed and docker-unsigned jobs that use docker:stable with docker:dind service, run only on tags/schedules (signed) or on non‑tag branches (unsigned), execute NIGHTLY check, then for signed run ./_scripts/ci/build_docker.sh, and for unsigned copy the built Linux amd64 tarball, login to $CI_REGISTRY, build a Docker image with build‑arg BINARY_NAME set to the tarball name, tag it as $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/unsigned:$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG and push it, both producing no artifacts. Add a demo_master job in the staging stage that uses google/cloud-sdk, runs a before_script installing gettext, writing $GOOGLE_KEY to key.json, authenticating with gcloud, setting zone, project and cluster, then runs envsubst on deployment/stp-review-deploy.yml and applies it with kubectl, limited to the master branch on an active Kubernetes cluster. Add lint-source in the test stage using golang:bullseye, allow failures, run gometalinter with config gometalinter.conf on the project directory, sort output into linter.log and store it as an artifact for two weeks. Add build-project in the test stage that clones the repo, checks out the commit, lists dependencies via ./_scripts/list_deps.sh, runs go get -d, then builds all packages with go build ./..., with no artifacts. Add package-source in the package stage that runs only on tags, executes NIGHTLY check, makes the source tarball via ./_scripts/ci/pack_source.sh, sets FILE_NAME to stp-src_${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}.tar.gz and stores it as artifact named stp-source. Add save-to-cubi in a custom save-to-cubi stage that depends on xgo and package-source, runs NIGHTLY check and then ./_scripts/ci/save_to_cubi.sh, with only tags, using golang:1.10. Finally add sonar-cloud in its own stage using niklaskhf/sonar-runner-docker, cloning the repo again, checking out the commit, then running sonar-scanner with project version from ./_scripts/ci/get_version.sh and authentication via $SONAR_CLOUD, uploading coverage from coverage.out.
